summary: Coding/code generation is not just the killer app for AI but may be the ultimate AI application and the shortest path to AGI
summary_extended: Baker argues that code generation is not merely the 'killer app' for AI but may be the single most important AI application and the most direct path to AGI/ASI, because writing code to autonomously accomplish tasks creates a self-reinforcing loop that accelerates intelligence development. He attributes the original framing to a post on X but endorses it strongly, contending that coding subsumes an ever-expanding range of applications over time as more domains become expressible in code. Practically, Baker recommends that even non-coders use tools like Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex to extract investment insights, suggesting these are already meaningfully useful today. The thesis is structural and long-term in nature, and Baker does not frame it as a specific portfolio position tied to a named equity. No specific financial metrics or timeframes are cited, making this primarily a thematic/directional view rather than an actionable single-stock call.
detail: Baker endorses the view that coding may be the shortest path to ASI/AGI because writing code to do things for yourself is a fast elegant path to AGI. He argues coding subsumes more and more applications over time. Practically recommends using Claude Code and Codex even for non-coders seeking investment insights.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as a forward-looking structural view but attributes the original insight to someone on X, and his own endorsement is strong but not framed as a portfolio position
quote_fragment: coding may be the shortest path to ASI and AGI
